journey.name: Bookflow — search, basket, seats, bags & SSR
journey.description: End-to-end booking journey via the Retail API: outbound flight search, return flight search, basket creation, passenger entry, seat selection, bag selection, and payment confirmation.

steps.name: Search outbound flights
steps.description: Search for available outbound flights from LHR to JFK for a single adult passenger on a Revenue booking. Returns a sessionId and a list of flights with cabin and fare-family offers. The sessionId and a selected outbound offerId are forwarded to the basket creation step.

steps.name: Search return flights
steps.description: Search for available return flights from JFK back to LHR for the same passenger on the return date. Returns a second sessionId and flight list. The sessionId and a selected return offerId are forwarded to the basket creation step alongside the outbound selections.

steps.name: Create basket
steps.description: Create a new basket containing the selected outbound and return flight offers. Each segment pairs an offerId with the sessionId from its respective search. The loyalty number is included so points can be accrued on confirmation. Returns a basketId used for all subsequent basket operations.

dataChain.source: Step 1 → random offer from search results
Step 2 → random offer from search results

steps.name: Get basket summary
steps.description: Reprice all offers in the basket and retrieve a pricing summary with tax line breakdowns. This call triggers the Offer MS to refresh fare amounts from live cabin occupancy and FareRule data, copies tax lines into the stored offer, and sets validated = true on each inventory entry — unblocking the confirmation step.

assertions.description: flights array must be present and non-empty.
Every flight in the summary must have validated = true after repricing.

steps.name: Get basket
steps.description: Retrieve the basket immediately after creation to verify the initial state. Confirms both flight offers are present as basket items and that the basket status is Active.

steps.name: Add passengers
steps.description: Add passenger details to the basket. Each passenger record includes personal details, contact information, and an optional loyalty number for points accrual. The passengerId (PAX-1, PAX-2 …) is assigned by the caller and must be consistent across seats and bags additions.

steps.name: Get basket (after passengers)
steps.description: Retrieve the basket after adding passenger details to confirm the passenger record is persisted in basketData.passengers.

steps.name: Get outbound seatmap
steps.description: Retrieve the seat map for the outbound flight using the inventoryId, flightNumber, aircraftType, and cabinCode chained from the basket. Passing cabinCode restricts the response to the booked cabin only. Returns a cabin-structured map of priced seat offers with availability status. A random available seat is selected from the response and its details chained to the add-seats step.

steps.name: Get return seatmap
steps.description: Retrieve the seat map for the return flight using the inventoryId, flightNumber, aircraftType, and cabinCode chained from the basket. Passing cabinCode restricts the response to the booked cabin only. A random available seat is selected and chained to the add-seats step.

steps.name: Add seats
steps.description: Add seat selections to the basket for each passenger on each flight segment. Seat details (seatOfferId, seatNumber, position, price, currency) are taken from the seatmap responses in Steps 7 and 8.

steps.name: Get basket (after seats)
steps.description: Retrieve the basket after adding seats to confirm both seat records are persisted in basketData.seats and that totalSeatAmount and totalAmount reflect the seat cost.

steps.name: Add bags
steps.description: Add additional baggage to the basket for each passenger on each segment. Each bag record references the passengerId, segmentId (basketItemRef of the segment), the bag offer identifier, the number of additional bags, the total price, and currency.

steps.name: Add SSRs — wheelchair assistance
steps.description: Store wheelchair assistance (WCHR) SSR selections on the basket for PAX-1 across all segments. SSRs carry no charge; the basket total is unchanged. Returns the basketId and SSR count.

steps.name: Confirm and pay — invalid card (Luhn failure)
steps.description: Attempt to confirm the basket with a card number that fails the Luhn check. The Payment service must reject the card details and return a 4xx error so the consumer can surface a meaningful message to the user. The basket must remain Active so payment can be retried.

assertions.description: Human-readable error message must be present so the consumer can surface it to the user.
Machine-readable error code must be present in the response body.

steps.name: Confirm and pay — valid card
steps.description: Submit payment with a card number that passes the Luhn check to confirm the basket and create the order. The basket transitions to Confirmed and an orderId is returned.

assertions.description: One e-ticket must be issued per passenger (matches the paxCount for this run). Unique e-ticket numbers are counted to avoid double-counting the same ticket appearing across multiple segment order items.
Every e-ticket must carry a non-empty eTicketNumber in the format '932-NNNNNNNNNN'.
